summary: Full end-to-end testing of new 'Book-club reading rooms' P2 feature. Backend (29/29 pytest cases) and frontend (all 13 critical UI flows) pass against live preview URL.
critical_code_review_comments: bookclubs.py is 654 lines — still manageable, but watch for growth; consider splitting into routers (rooms / members / messages / progress) if more endpoints are added.
Initial 401 console error on first /bookclubs visit is from the auth bootstrap before cookie is set (expected pattern in this app); ignore.
RoleBadge ordering inside list page lacks 'invited' state, but that path is only reachable via the dedicated invites strip — acceptable.
BookclubRoomPage useEffect that syncs URL when activeChapter changes ignores ESLint deps intentionally — fine, but document via comment.
test_credentials: Seeded ephemeral users/sessions in Mongo (Bearer-token + cookie injection). Cleaned up after run. test_bookclubs.py manages its own teardown.
seed_data_creation: Created two transient users (owner+friend), one book with 3 chapters, and an accepted friendship for UI testing. All seeded data was deleted post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_bookclubs.py is parameterized via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and seeds/cleans its own data. For UI, set httpOnly session_token cookie on context with domain=genre-sort.preview.emergentagent.com to bypass OAuth. Owner-only UI gating (edit/delete/invite-card) was verified for member role.
tests_executed: Backend: TestBookclubCreateAndList (5), TestBookclubInviteFlow (8), TestRoomEditAndRoles (4), TestMessages (6), TestProgressAndMilestones (3), TestTransferAndLeave (3) — 29/29 passed

Frontend: empty state, create-room form, room detail load, chapter tabs (4 tabs for 3-chapter book), URL search param sync (?chapter=1), send message via button, send via Ctrl+Enter, invite friend from staff panel, progress slider, member list rendering, friend pending-invite strip + accept flow, role-based gating (member sees only Leave; no edit/delete/invite), /friends -> friends-to-bookclubs link, /account -> privacy-open-bookclubs-btn
